title: Attributing mortality from extreme temperatures to climate change in Stockholm, Sweden

meta_data.Data_Name: Stockholm daily air temperature observations and homogenized series
meta_data.Data_summary: Station-based daily mean air temperature observations for Stockholm compiled into a long time series and used to compute moving averages and percentile-based thresholds for defining cold and heat extremes and counting their frequency across periods, including versions corrected for urbanization and urban heat island effects.
meta_data.Category: City-wide camera networks and meteorological stations
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We collected daily mortality during the period 1980–2009 and daily temperature data for the period 1900–2009 for Stockholm County Sweden; Location: Methods; Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Stockholm County daily all cause mortality counts
meta_data.Data_summary: Daily counts of deaths for Stockholm County used as the health outcome time series to estimate relative risks of mortality during defined cold and heat extreme days and to compute baseline seasonal mortality for attributable-death calculations.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We collected daily mortality during the period 1980–2009 and daily temperature data for the period 1900–2009 for Stockholm County Sweden; Location: Methods; Confidence: high
